The '''Noolan''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]' [[Noola system/Legends|Noola system]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Noolan [[Otolia Ratiuv]], a [[Technician/Legends|technician]], found her body occupied by the essence of [[Nana]], a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]]-affiliated member of the [[Homanan]] species, following an explosion near a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Force crystal]]. Nana gained [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]] from the [[Transfer essence|transfer]] and continued serving in the Alliance to Restore the Republic in her new body.

==Biology and appearance==
The Noolan were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that had [[Gender/Legends|females]].<ref name="Poly 59" />

==History==
The Noolan<ref name="Poly 59" /> evolved in the [[Noola system/Legends|Noola system]].<ref name="TEAOC" /><ref name="Planetnamia" /> Their [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] [[Star system/Legends|system]] was part of the [[Relgim sector/Legends|Relgim sector]], an area of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEAOC" />

==Noolan in the galaxy==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Otolia Ratiuv]], a female Noolan [[Technician/Legends|technician]], was near an explosion that [[Death/Legends|killed]] the body of a nearby [[Homanan]]. Due to the coincidental proximity of a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Force crystal]], the Homanan, [[Nana]], was able to [[Transfer essence|transfer her essence]] into Ratiuv. During this process Nana lost the [[Changeling/Legends|shape-shifting]] ability of the Homanans, but she instead developed [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]]. In her new Noolan body, Nana continued agitating for a change in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] governance through the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="Poly 59" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Joe Littrell]] created the Noolan for the article "[[The New Rogues Gallery: Unsung Heroes of the Rebellion]]," a roleplaying game supplement published in ''[[Polyhedron 59]]'' in [[1991]]. [[Leland Chee]], has been unable to confirm whether certain publications, ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine included, were officially affiliated with [[Lucasfilm]], nor confirm [[Lucas Licensing]] arrangements prior to his tenure at [[Lucasfilm]],<ref name="SWB">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=1575#14229569|thread=Welcome some BCaT VIPs|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-02-28|time=6:28 PM}}</ref> which has left the [[canon]] status of the Noolan ambiguous. Despite the uncertainty surrounding the species's place in continuity, the Noola system is referenced in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', published in [[2009]].<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> However, according to one of the authors, [[Jason Fry]], "its inclusion shouldn't be taken as a statement of canonicity for anything except the geography."<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/29648348|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2009-01-04|time=7:52 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200714135715/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-atlas-and-galactic-cartography-official-discussion.21938930/page-72}}</ref> ''Polyhedron 59'' mentioned Noolan only in the singular form, so no plural for the species has yet been specified.
